<pr_description>
BUG: Variable not defined when awaited and rendered inside a snippet
### Describe the bug

I’m using the experimental `async` flag.

When rendering an awaited variable inside a snippet — without explicitly declaring it in the snippet parameters — the console throws the following error:

```
Uncaught (in promise) ReferenceError: value is not defined
```

I’m currently on **v5.38.3**. This was working correctly in **v5.38.2**.


### Reproduction

https://stackblitz.com/edit/sveltejs-kit-template-default-ry9pp9kr?file=src%2Froutes%2F%2Bpage.svelte

     1	/** @import { AssignmentPattern, BlockStatement, Expression, Identifier, Statement } from 'estree' */
     2	/** @import { AST } from '#compiler' */
     3	/** @import { ComponentContext } from '../types' */
     4	import { dev } from '../../../../state.js';
     5	import { extract_paths } from '../../../../utils/ast.js';
     6	import * as b from '#compiler/builders';
     7	import { get_value } from './shared/declarations.js';
     8	
     9	/**
    10	 * @param {AST.SnippetBlock} node
    11	 * @param {ComponentContext} context
    12	 */
    13	export function SnippetBlock(node, context) {
    14	        // TODO hoist where possible
    15	        /** @type {(Identifier | AssignmentPattern)[]} */
    16	        const args = [b.id('$$anchor')];
    17	        const has_await = node.body.metadata.has_await || false;
    18	
    19	        /** @type {BlockStatement} */
    20	        let body;
    21	
    22	        /** @type {Statement[]} */
    23	        const declarations = [];
    24	
    25	        const transform = { ...context.state.transform };
    26	        const child_state = { ...context.state, transform };
    27	
    28	        for (let i = 0; i < node.parameters.length; i++) {
    29	                const argument = node.parameters[i];
    30	
    31	                if (!argument) continue;
    32	
    33	                if (argument.type === 'Identifier') {
    34	                        args.push(b.assignment_pattern(argument, b.id('$.noop')));
    35	                        transform[argument.name] = { read: b.call };
    36	
    37	                        continue;
    38	                }
    39	
    40	                let arg_alias = `$$arg${i}`;
    41	                args.push(b.id(arg_alias));
    42	
    43	                const { inserts, paths } = extract_paths(argument, b.maybe_call(b.id(arg_alias)));
    44	
    45	                for (const { id, value } of inserts) {
    46	                        id.name = context.state.scope.generate('$$array');
    47	                        transform[id.name] = { read: get_value };
    48	
    49	                        declarations.push(
    50	                                b.var(id, b.call('$.derived', /** @type {Expression} */ (context.visit(b.thunk(value)))))
    51	                        );
    52	                }
    53	
    54	                for (const path of paths) {
    55	                        const name = /** @type {Identifier} */ (path.node).name;
    56	                        const needs_derived = path.has_default_value; // to ensure that default value is only called once
    57	                        const fn = b.thunk(/** @type {Expression} */ (context.visit(path.expression, child_state)));
    58	
    59	                        declarations.push(b.let(path.node, needs_derived ? b.call('$.derived_safe_equal', fn) : fn));
    60	
    61	                        transform[name] = {
    62	                                read: needs_derived ? get_value : b.call
    63	                        };
    64	
    65	                        // we need to eagerly evaluate the expression in order to hit any
    66	                        // 'Cannot access x before initialization' errors
    67	                        if (dev) {
    68	                                declarations.push(b.stmt(transform[name].read(b.id(name))));
    69	                        }
    70	                }
    71	        }
    72	        const block = /** @type {BlockStatement} */ (context.visit(node.body, child_state)).body;
    73	        body = b.block([
    74	                dev ? b.stmt(b.call('$.validate_snippet_args', b.spread(b.id('arguments')))) : b.empty,
    75	                ...declarations,
    76	                ...block
    77	        ]);
    78	
    79	        // in dev we use a FunctionExpression (not arrow function) so we can use `arguments`
    80	        let snippet = dev
    81	                ? b.call(
    82	                                '$.wrap_snippet',
    83	                                b.id(context.state.analysis.name),
    84	                                b.function(null, args, body, has_await)
    85	                        )
    86	                : b.arrow(args, body, has_await);
    87	
    88	        const declaration = b.const(node.expression, snippet);
    89	
    90	        // Top-level snippets are hoisted so they can be referenced in the `<script>`
    91	        if (context.path.length === 1 && context.path[0].type === 'Fragment') {
    92	                if (node.metadata.can_hoist) {
    93	                        context.state.module_level_snippets.push(declaration);
    94	                } else {
    95	                        context.state.instance_level_snippets.push(declaration);
    96	                }
    97	        } else {
    98	                context.state.init.push(declaration);
    99	        }
   100	}

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/packages/svelte/src/compiler/phases/3-transform/client/visitors/RenderTag.js:
     1	/** @import { Expression, Statement } from 'estree' */
     2	/** @import { AST } from '#compiler' */
     3	/** @import { ComponentContext } from '../types' */
     4	import { unwrap_optional } from '../../../../utils/ast.js';
     5	import * as b from '#compiler/builders';
     6	import { add_svelte_meta, build_expression, Memoizer } from './shared/utils.js';
     7	
     8	/**
     9	 * @param {AST.RenderTag} node
    10	 * @param {ComponentContext} context
    11	 */
    12	export function RenderTag(node, context) {
    13	        context.state.template.push_comment();
    14	
    15	        const call = unwrap_optional(node.expression);
    16	
    17	        /** @type {Expression[]} */
    18	        let args = [];
    19	
    20	        const memoizer = new Memoizer();
    21	
    22	        for (let i = 0; i < call.arguments.length; i++) {
    23	                const arg = /** @type {Expression} */ (call.arguments[i]);
    24	                const metadata = node.metadata.arguments[i];
    25	
    26	                let expression = build_expression(context, arg, metadata);
    27	
    28	                if (metadata.has_await || metadata.has_call) {
    29	                        expression = b.call('$.get', memoizer.add(expression, metadata.has_await));
    30	                }
    31	
    32	                args.push(b.thunk(expression));
    33	        }
    34	
    35	        memoizer.apply();
    36	
    37	        /** @type {Statement[]} */
    38	        const statements = memoizer.deriveds(context.state.analysis.runes);
    39	
    40	        let snippet_function = build_expression(
    41	                context,
    42	                /** @type {Expression} */ (call.callee),
    43	                node.metadata.expression
    44	        );
    45	
    46	        if (node.metadata.dynamic) {
    47	                // If we have a chain expression then ensure a nullish snippet function gets turned into an empty one
    48	                if (node.expression.type === 'ChainExpression') {
    49	                        snippet_function = b.logical('??', snippet_function, b.id('$.noop'));
    50	                }
    51	
    52	                statements.push(
    53	                        add_svelte_meta(
    54	                                b.call('$.snippet', context.state.node, b.thunk(snippet_function), ...args),
    55	                                node,
    56	                                'render'
    57	                        )
    58	                );
    59	        } else {
    60	                statements.push(
    61	                        add_svelte_meta(
    62	                                (node.expression.type === 'CallExpression' ? b.call : b.maybe_call)(
    63	                                        snippet_function,
    64	                                        context.state.node,
    65	                                        ...args
    66	                                ),
    67	                                node,
    68	                                'render'
    69	                        )
    70	                );
    71	        }
    72	
    73	        const async_values = memoizer.async_values();
    74	
    75	        if (async_values) {
    76	                context.state.init.push(
    77	                        b.stmt(
    78	                                b.call(
    79	                                        '$.async',
    80	                                        context.state.node,
    81	                                        memoizer.async_values(),
    82	                                        b.arrow([context.state.node, ...memoizer.async_ids()], b.block(statements))
    83	                                )
    84	                        )
    85	                );
    86	        } else {
    87	                context.state.init.push(statements.length === 1 ? statements[0] : b.block(statements));
    88	        }
    89	}

     9	import is_reference from 'is-reference';
    10	import { dev, is_ignored, locator, component_name } from '../../../../../state.js';

    13	/**
    14	 * A utility for extracting complex expressions (such as call expressions)
    15	 * from templates and replacing them with `$0`, `$1` etc
    16	 */
    17	export class Memoizer {
    18	        /** @type {Array<{ id: Identifier, expression: Expression }>} */
    19	        #sync = [];
    20	
    21	        /** @type {Array<{ id: Identifier, expression: Expression }>} */
    22	        #async = [];
    23	
    24	        /**
    25	         * @param {Expression} expression
    26	         * @param {boolean} has_await
    27	         */
    28	        add(expression, has_await) {
    29	                const id = b.id('#'); // filled in later
    30	
    31	                (has_await ? this.#async : this.#sync).push({ id, expression });
    32	
    33	                return id;
    34	        }
    35	
    36	        apply() {
    37	                return [...this.#sync, ...this.#async].map((memo, i) => {
    38	                        memo.id.name = `$${i}`;
    39	                        return memo.id;
    40	                });
    41	        }
    42	
    43	        deriveds(runes = true) {
    44	                return this.#sync.map((memo) =>
    45	                        b.let(memo.id, b.call(runes ? '$.derived' : '$.derived_safe_equal', b.thunk(memo.expression)))
    46	                );
    47	        }
    48	
    49	        async_ids() {
    50	                return this.#async.map((memo) => memo.id);
    51	        }
    52	
    53	        async_values() {
    54	                if (this.#async.length === 0) return;
    55	                return b.array(this.#async.map((memo) => b.thunk(memo.expression, true)));
    56	        }
    57	
    58	        sync_values() {
    59	                if (this.#sync.length === 0) return;
    60	                return b.array(this.#sync.map((memo) => b.thunk(memo.expression)));
    61	        }
    62	}
    63	
    64	/**
    65	 * @param {Array<AST.Text | AST.ExpressionTag>} values
    66	 * @param {ComponentContext} context
    67	 * @param {ComponentClientTransformState} state
    68	 * @param {(value: Expression, metadata: ExpressionMetadata) => Expression} memoize
    69	 * @returns {{ value: Expression, has_state: boolean }}
    70	 */
    71	export function build_template_chunk(
    72	        values,
    73	        context,
    74	        state = context.state,
    75	        memoize = (value, metadata) =>
    76	                metadata.has_call || metadata.has_await ? state.memoizer.add(value, metadata.has_await) : value
    77	) {
    78	        /** @type {Expression[]} */
    79	        const expressions = [];
    80	
    81	        let quasi = b.quasi('');
    82	        const quasis = [quasi];
    83	
    84	        let has_state = false;
    85	        let has_await = false;
    86	
    87	        for (let i = 0; i < values.length; i++) {
    88	                const node = values[i];
    89	
    90	                if (node.type === 'Text') {
    91	                        quasi.value.cooked += node.data;
    92	                } else if (node.expression.type === 'Literal') {
    93	                        if (node.expression.value != null) {
    94	                                quasi.value.cooked += node.expression.value + '';
    95	                        }
    96	                } else if (
    97	                        node.expression.type !== 'Identifier' ||
    98	                        node.expression.name !== 'undefined' ||
    99	                        state.scope.get('undefined')
   100	                ) {
   101	                        let value = memoize(
   102	                                build_expression(context, node.expression, node.metadata.expression, state),
   103	                                node.metadata.expression
   104	                        );
   105	
   106	                        const evaluated = state.scope.evaluate(value);
   107	
   108	                        has_await ||= node.metadata.expression.has_await;
   109	                        has_state ||= has_await || (node.metadata.expression.has_state && !evaluated.is_known);
   110	
   111	                        if (values.length === 1) {
   112	                                // If we have a single expression, then pass that in directly to possibly avoid doing
   113	                                // extra work in the template_effect (instead we do the work in set_text).
   114	                                if (evaluated.is_known) {
   115	                                        value = b.literal((evaluated.value ?? '') + '');
   116	                                }
   117	
   118	                                return { value, has_state };
   119	                        }
   120	
   121	                        if (
   122	                                value.type === 'LogicalExpression' &&
   123	                                value.right.type === 'Literal' &&
   124	                                (value.operator === '??' || value.operator === '||')
   125	                        ) {
   126	                                // `foo ?? null` -=> `foo ?? ''`
   127	                                // otherwise leave the expression untouched
   128	                                if (value.right.value === null) {
   129	                                        value = { ...value, right: b.literal('') };
   130	                                }
   131	                        }
   132	
   133	                        if (evaluated.is_known) {
   134	                                quasi.value.cooked += (evaluated.value ?? '') + '';
   135	                        } else {
   136	                                if (!evaluated.is_defined) {
   137	                                        // add `?? ''` where necessary
   138	                                        value = b.logical('??', value, b.literal(''));
   139	                                }
   140	
   141	                                expressions.push(value);
   142	
   143	                                quasi = b.quasi('', i + 1 === values.length);
   144	                                quasis.push(quasi);
   145	                        }
   146	                }
   147	        }
   148	
   149	        for (const quasi of quasis) {
   150	                quasi.value.raw = sanitize_template_string(/** @type {string} */ (quasi.value.cooked));
   151	        }
   152	
   153	        const value =
   154	                expressions.length > 0
   155	                        ? b.template(quasis, expressions)
   156	                        : b.literal(/** @type {string} */ (quasi.value.cooked));
   157	
   158	        return { value, has_state };
   159	}

   161	/**
   162	 * @param {ComponentClientTransformState} state
   163	 */
   164	export function build_render_statement(state) {
   165	        const { memoizer } = state;
   166	
   167	        const ids = memoizer.apply();
   168	
   169	        return b.stmt(
   170	                b.call(
   171	                        '$.template_effect',
   172	                        b.arrow(
   173	                                ids,
   174	                                state.update.length === 1 && state.update[0].type === 'ExpressionStatement'
   175	                                        ? state.update[0].expression
   176	                                        : b.block(state.update)
   177	                        ),
   178	                        memoizer.sync_values(),
   179	                        memoizer.async_values()
   180	                )
   181	        );
   182	}
   183	
   184	/**
   185	 * For unfortunate legacy reasons, directive names can look like this `use:a.b-c`
   186	 * This turns that string into a member expression
   187	 * @param {string} name
   188	 */
   189	export function parse_directive_name(name) {
   190	        // this allow for accessing members of an object
   191	        const parts = name.split('.');
   192	        let part = /** @type {string} */ (parts.shift());
   193	
   194	        /** @type {Identifier | MemberExpression} */
   195	        let expression = b.id(part);
   196	
   197	        while ((part = /** @type {string} */ (parts.shift()))) {
   198	                const computed = !regex_is_valid_identifier.test(part);
   199	                expression = b.member(expression, computed ? b.literal(part) : b.id(part), computed);
   200	        }
   201	
   202	        return expression;
   203	}
   204	
   205	/**
   206	 * Serializes `bind:this` for components and elements.
   207	 * @param {Identifier | MemberExpression | SequenceExpression} expression
   208	 * @param {Expression} value
   209	 * @param {import('zimmerframe').Context<AST.SvelteNode, ComponentClientTransformState>} context
   210	 */
   211	export function build_bind_this(expression, value, { state, visit }) {
   212	        if (expression.type === 'SequenceExpression') {
   213	                const [get, set] = /** @type {SequenceExpression} */ (visit(expression)).expressions;
   214	                return b.call('$.bind_this', value, set, get);
   215	        }
   216	
   217	        /** @type {Identifier[]} */
   218	        const ids = [];
   219	
   220	        /** @type {Expression[]} */
   221	        const values = [];
   222	
   223	        /** @type {string[]} */
   224	        const seen = [];
   225	
   226	        const transform = { ...state.transform };
   227	
   228	        // Pass in each context variables to the get/set functions, so that we can null out old values on teardown.
   229	        // Note that we only do this for each context variables, the consequence is that the value might be stale in
   230	        // some scenarios where the value is a member expression with changing computed parts or using a combination of multiple
   231	        // variables, but that was the same case in Svelte 4, too. Once legacy mode is gone completely, we can revisit this.
   232	        walk(expression, null, {
   233	                Identifier(node, { path }) {
   234	                        if (seen.includes(node.name)) return;
   235	                        seen.push(node.name);
   236	
   237	                        const parent = /** @type {Expression} */ (path.at(-1));
   238	                        if (!is_reference(node, parent)) return;
   239	
   240	                        const binding = state.scope.get(node.name);
   241	                        if (!binding) return;
   242	
   243	                        for (const [owner, scope] of state.scopes) {
   244	                                if (owner.type === 'EachBlock' && scope === binding.scope) {
   245	                                        ids.push(node);
   246	                                        values.push(/** @type {Expression} */ (visit(node)));
   247	
   248	                                        if (transform[node.name]) {
   249	                                                transform[node.name] = {
   250	                                                        ...transform[node.name],
   251	                                                        read: (node) => node
   252	                                                };
   253	                                        }
   254	
   255	                                        break;
   256	                                }
   257	                        }
   258	                }
   259	        });
   260	
   261	        const child_state = { ...state, transform };
   262	
   263	        const get = /** @type {Expression} */ (visit(expression, child_state));
   264	        const set = /** @type {Expression} */ (
   265	                visit(b.assignment('=', expression, b.id('$$value')), child_state)
   266	        );
   267	
   268	        // If we're mutating a property, then it might already be non-existent.
   269	        // If we make all the object nodes optional, then it avoids any runtime exceptions.
   270	        /** @type {Expression | Super} */
   271	        let node = get;
   272	
   273	        while (node.type === 'MemberExpression') {
   274	                node.optional = true;
   275	                node = node.object;
   276	        }
   277	
   278	        return b.call(
   279	                '$.bind_this',
   280	                value,
   281	                b.arrow([b.id('$$value'), ...ids], set),
   282	                b.arrow([...ids], get),
   283	                values.length > 0 && b.thunk(b.array(values))
   284	        );
   285	}
   286	
   287	/**
   288	 * @param {ComponentClientTransformState} state
   289	 * @param {AST.BindDirective} binding
   290	 * @param {MemberExpression} expression
   291	 */
   292	export function validate_binding(state, binding, expression) {
   293	        if (binding.expression.type === 'SequenceExpression') {
   294	                return;
   295	        }
   296	        // If we are referencing a $store.foo then we don't need to add validation
   297	        const left = object(binding.expression);
   298	        const left_binding = left && state.scope.get(left.name);
   299	        if (left_binding?.kind === 'store_sub') return;
   300	
   301	        const loc = locator(binding.start);
   302	
   303	        const obj = /** @type {Expression} */ (expression.object);
   304	
   305	        state.init.push(
   306	                b.stmt(
   307	                        b.call(
   308	                                '$.validate_binding',
   309	                                b.literal(state.analysis.source.slice(binding.start, binding.end)),
   310	                                b.thunk(
   311	                                        state.store_to_invalidate ? b.sequence([b.call('$.mark_store_binding'), obj]) : obj
   312	                                ),
   313	                                b.thunk(
   314	                                        /** @type {Expression} */ (
   315	                                                expression.computed
   316	                                                        ? expression.property
   317	                                                        : b.literal(/** @type {Identifier} */ (expression.property).name)
   318	                                        )
   319	                                ),
   320	                                loc && b.literal(loc.line),
   321	                                loc && b.literal(loc.column)
   322	                        )
   323	                )
   324	        );
   325	}
   326	
   327	/**
   328	 * In dev mode validate mutations to props
   329	 * @param {AssignmentExpression | UpdateExpression} node
   330	 * @param {Context} context
   331	 * @param {Expression} expression
   332	 */
   333	export function validate_mutation(node, context, expression) {
   334	        let left = /** @type {Expression | Super} */ (
   335	                node.type === 'AssignmentExpression' ? node.left : node.argument
   336	        );
   337	
   338	        if (!dev || left.type !== 'MemberExpression' || is_ignored(node, 'ownership_invalid_mutation')) {
   339	                return expression;
   340	        }
   341	
   342	        const name = object(left);
   343	        if (!name) return expression;
   344	
   345	        const binding = context.state.scope.get(name.name);
   346	        if (binding?.kind !== 'prop' && binding?.kind !== 'bindable_prop') return expression;
   347	
   348	        const state = /** @type {ComponentClientTransformState} */ (context.state);
   349	        state.analysis.needs_mutation_validation = true;
   350	
   351	        /** @type {Array<Identifier | Literal | Expression>} */
   352	        const path = [];
   353	
   354	        while (left.type === 'MemberExpression') {
   355	                if (left.property.type === 'Literal') {
   356	                        path.unshift(left.property);
   357	                } else if (left.property.type === 'Identifier') {
   358	                        const transform = Object.hasOwn(context.state.transform, left.property.name)
   359	                                ? context.state.transform[left.property.name]
   360	                                : null;
   361	                        if (left.computed) {
   362	                                path.unshift(transform?.read ? transform.read(left.property) : left.property);
   363	                        } else {
   364	                                path.unshift(b.literal(left.property.name));
   365	                        }
   366	                } else {
   367	                        return expression;
   368	                }
   369	
   370	                left = left.object;
   371	        }
   372	
   373	        path.unshift(b.literal(name.name));
   374	
   375	        const loc = locator(/** @type {number} */ (left.start));
   376	
   377	        return b.call(
   378	                '$$ownership_validator.mutation',
   379	                b.literal(binding.prop_alias),
   380	                b.array(path),
   381	                expression,
   382	                loc && b.literal(loc.line),
   383	                loc && b.literal(loc.column)
   384	        );
   385	}
   386	
   387	/**
   388	 *
   389	 * @param {ComponentContext} context
   390	 * @param {Expression} expression
   391	 * @param {ExpressionMetadata} metadata
   392	 */
   393	export function build_expression(context, expression, metadata, state = context.state) {
   394	        const value = /** @type {Expression} */ (context.visit(expression, state));
   395	
   396	        // Components not explicitly in legacy mode might be expected to be in runes mode (especially since we didn't
   397	        // adjust this behavior until recently, which broke people's existing components), so we also bail in this case.
   398	        // Kind of an in-between-mode.
   399	        if (context.state.analysis.runes || context.state.analysis.maybe_runes) {
   400	                return value;
   401	        }
   402	
   403	        if (!metadata.has_call && !metadata.has_member_expression && !metadata.has_assignment) {
   404	                return value;
   405	        }
   406	
   407	        // Legacy reactivity is coarse-grained, looking at the statically visible dependencies. Replicate that here
   408	        const sequence = b.sequence([]);
   409	
   410	        for (const binding of metadata.references) {
   411	                if (binding.kind === 'normal' && binding.declaration_kind !== 'import') {
   412	                        continue;
   413	                }
   414	
   415	                var getter = build_getter({ ...binding.node }, state);
   416	
   417	                if (
   418	                        binding.kind === 'bindable_prop' ||
   419	                        binding.kind === 'template' ||
   420	                        binding.declaration_kind === 'import' ||
   421	                        binding.node.name === '$$props' ||
   422	                        binding.node.name === '$$restProps'
   423	                ) {
   424	                        getter = b.call('$.deep_read_state', getter);
   425	                }
   426	
   427	                sequence.expressions.push(getter);
   428	        }
   429	
   430	        sequence.expressions.push(b.call('$.untrack', b.thunk(value)));
   431	
   432	        return sequence;
   433	}
   434	
   435	/**
   436	 * Wraps a statement/expression with dev stack tracking in dev mode
   437	 * @param {Expression} expression - The function call to wrap (e.g., $.if, $.each, etc.)
   438	 * @param {{ start?: number }} node - AST node for location info
   439	 * @param {'component' | 'if' | 'each' | 'await' | 'key' | 'render'} type - Type of block/component
   440	 * @param {Record<string, number | string>} [additional] - Any additional properties to add to the dev stack entry
   441	 * @returns {ExpressionStatement} - Statement with or without dev stack wrapping
   442	 */
   443	export function add_svelte_meta(expression, node, type, additional) {
   444	        if (!dev) {
   445	                return b.stmt(expression);
   446	        }
   447	
   448	        const location = node.start !== undefined && locator(node.start);
   449	        if (!location) {
   450	                return b.stmt(expression);
   451	        }
   452	
   453	        return b.stmt(
   454	                b.call(
   455	                        '$.add_svelte_meta',
   456	                        b.arrow([], expression),
   457	                        b.literal(type),
   458	                        b.id(component_name),
   459	                        b.literal(location.line),
   460	                        b.literal(location.column),
   461	                        additional && b.object(Object.entries(additional).map(([k, v]) => b.init(k, b.literal(v))))
   462	                )
   463	        );
   464	}

     2	/** @import { Effect, TemplateNode } from '#client' */
     3	/** @import { Getters } from '#shared' */
     4	import { EFFECT_TRANSPARENT, ELEMENT_NODE } from '#client/constants';
     5	import { branch, block, destroy_effect, teardown } from '../../reactivity/effects.js';
     6	import {
     7	        dev_current_component_function,
     8	        set_dev_current_component_function
     9	} from '../../context.js';
    10	import { hydrate_next, hydrate_node, hydrating } from '../hydration.js';
    11	import { create_fragment_from_html } from '../reconciler.js';
    12	import { assign_nodes } from '../template.js';
    13	import * as w from '../../warnings.js';
    14	import * as e from '../../errors.js';
    15	import { DEV } from 'esm-env';
    16	import { get_first_child, get_next_sibling } from '../operations.js';
    17	import { noop } from '../../../shared/utils.js';
    18	import { prevent_snippet_stringification } from '../../../shared/validate.js';
    19	
    20	/**
    21	 * @template {(node: TemplateNode, ...args: any[]) => void} SnippetFn
    22	 * @param {TemplateNode} node
    23	 * @param {() => SnippetFn | null | undefined} get_snippet
    24	 * @param {(() => any)[]} args
    25	 * @returns {void}
    26	 */
    27	export function snippet(node, get_snippet, ...args) {
    28	        var anchor = node;
    29	
    30	        /** @type {SnippetFn | null | undefined} */
    31	        // @ts-ignore
    32	        var snippet = noop;
    33	
    34	        /** @type {Effect | null} */
    35	        var snippet_effect;
    36	
    37	        block(() => {
    38	                if (snippet === (snippet = get_snippet())) return;
    39	
    40	                if (snippet_effect) {
    41	                        destroy_effect(snippet_effect);
    42	                        snippet_effect = null;
    43	                }
    44	
    45	                if (DEV && snippet == null) {
    46	                        e.invalid_snippet();
    47	                }
    48	
    49	                snippet_effect = branch(() => /** @type {SnippetFn} */ (snippet)(anchor, ...args));
    50	        }, EFFECT_TRANSPARENT);
    51	
    52	        if (hydrating) {
    53	                anchor = hydrate_node;
    54	        }
    55	}
    56	
    57	/**
    58	 * In development, wrap the snippet function so that it passes validation, and so that the
    59	 * correct component context is set for ownership checks
    60	 * @param {any} component
    61	 * @param {(node: TemplateNode, ...args: any[]) => void} fn
    62	 */
    63	export function wrap_snippet(component, fn) {
    64	        const snippet = (/** @type {TemplateNode} */ node, /** @type {any[]} */ ...args) => {
    65	                var previous_component_function = dev_current_component_function;
    66	                set_dev_current_component_function(component);
    67	
    68	                try {
    69	                        return fn(node, ...args);
    70	                } finally {
    71	                        set_dev_current_component_function(previous_component_function);
    72	                }
    73	        };
    74	
    75	        prevent_snippet_stringification(snippet);
    76	
    77	        return snippet;
    78	}
    79	
    80	/**
    81	 * Create a snippet programmatically
    82	 * @template {unknown[]} Params
    83	 * @param {(...params: Getters<Params>) => {
    84	 *   render: () => string
    85	 *   setup?: (element: Element) => void | (() => void)
    86	 * }} fn
    87	 * @returns {Snippet<Params>}
    88	 */
    89	export function createRawSnippet(fn) {
    90	        // @ts-expect-error the types are a lie
    91	        return (/** @type {TemplateNode} */ anchor, /** @type {Getters<Params>} */ ...params) => {
    92	                var snippet = fn(...params);
    93	
    94	                /** @type {Element} */
    95	                var element;
    96	
    97	                if (hydrating) {
    98	                        element = /** @type {Element} */ (hydrate_node);
    99	                        hydrate_next();
   100	                } else {
   101	                        var html = snippet.render().trim();
   102	                        var fragment = create_fragment_from_html(html);
   103	                        element = /** @type {Element} */ (get_first_child(fragment));
   104	
   105	                        if (DEV && (get_next_sibling(element) !== null || element.nodeType !== ELEMENT_NODE)) {
   106	                                w.invalid_raw_snippet_render();
   107	                        }
   108	
   109	                        anchor.before(element);
   110	                }
   111	
   112	                const result = snippet.setup?.(element);
   113	                assign_nodes(element, element);
   114	
   115	                if (typeof result === 'function') {
   116	                        teardown(result);
   117	                }
   118	        };
   119	}
   120

     2	import { flatten } from '../../reactivity/async.js';
     3	import { get } from '../../runtime.js';
     4	import { get_pending_boundary } from './boundary.js';
     5	
     6	/**
     7	 * @param {TemplateNode} node
     8	 * @param {Array<() => Promise<any>>} expressions
     9	 * @param {(anchor: TemplateNode, ...deriveds: Value[]) => void} fn
    10	 */
    11	export function async(node, expressions, fn) {
    12	        var boundary = get_pending_boundary();
    13	
    14	        boundary.update_pending_count(1);
    15	
    16	        flatten([], expressions, (values) => {
    17	                try {
    18	                        // get values eagerly to avoid creating blocks if they reject
    19	                        for (const d of values) get(d);
    20

    22	                } finally {
    23	                        boundary.update_pending_count(-1);
    24	                }
    25	        });

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/packages/svelte/src/compiler/phases/3-transform/client/visitors/shared/component.js:
     1	/** @import { BlockStatement, Expression, ExpressionStatement, Identifier, MemberExpression, Pattern, Property, SequenceExpression, Statement } from 'estree' */
     2	/** @import { AST } from '#compiler' */
     3	/** @import { ComponentContext } from '../../types.js' */
     4	import { dev, is_ignored } from '../../../../../state.js';
     5	import { get_attribute_chunks, object } from '../../../../../utils/ast.js';
     6	import * as b from '#compiler/builders';
     7	import { add_svelte_meta, build_bind_this, Memoizer, validate_binding } from '../shared/utils.js';
     8	import { build_attribute_value } from '../shared/element.js';
     9	import { build_event_handler } from './events.js';
    10	import { determine_slot } from '../../../../../utils/slot.js';
    11	
    12	/**
    13	 * @param {AST.Component | AST.SvelteComponent | AST.SvelteSelf} node
    14	 * @param {string} component_name
    15	 * @param {ComponentContext} context
    16	 * @returns {Statement}
    17	 */
    18	export function build_component(node, component_name, context) {
    19	        /**
    20	         * @type {Expression}
    21	         */
    22	        const anchor = context.state.node;
    23	        /** @type {Array<Property[] | Expression>} */
    24	        const props_and_spreads = [];
    25	        /** @type {Array<() => void>} */
    26	        const delayed_props = [];
    27	
    28	        /** @type {ExpressionStatement[]} */
    29	        const lets = [];
    30	
    31	        /** @type {Record<string, typeof context.state>} */
    32	        const states = {
    33	                default: {
    34	                        ...context.state,
    35	                        scope: node.metadata.scopes.default,
    36	                        transform: { ...context.state.transform }
    37	                }
    38	        };
    39	
    40	        /** @type {Record<string, AST.TemplateNode[]>} */
    41	        const children = {};
    42	
    43	        /** @type {Record<string, Expression[]>} */
    44	        const events = {};
    45	
    46	        const memoizer = new Memoizer();
    47	
    48	        /** @type {Property[]} */
    49	        const custom_css_props = [];
    50	
    51	        /** @type {Identifier | MemberExpression | SequenceExpression | null} */
    52	        let bind_this = null;
    53	
    54	        /** @type {ExpressionStatement[]} */
    55	        const binding_initializers = [];
    56	
    57	        const is_component_dynamic =
    58	                node.type === 'SvelteComponent' || (node.type === 'Component' && node.metadata.dynamic);
    59	
    60	        // The variable name used for the component inside $.component()
    61	        const intermediate_name =
    62	                node.type === 'Component' && node.metadata.dynamic
    63	                        ? context.state.scope.generate(node.name)
    64	                        : '$$component';
    65	
    66	        /**
    67	         * If this component has a slot property, it is a named slot within another component. In this case
    68	         * the slot scope applies to the component itself, too, and not just its children.
    69	         */
    70	        let slot_scope_applies_to_itself = !!determine_slot(node);
    71	
    72	        /**
    73	         * Components may have a children prop and also have child nodes. In this case, we assume
    74	         * that the child component isn't using render tags yet and pass the slot as $$slots.default.
    75	         * We're not doing it for spread attributes, as this would result in too many false positives.
    76	         */
    77	        let has_children_prop = false;
    78	
    79	        /**
    80	         * @param {Property} prop
    81	         * @param {boolean} [delay]
    82	         */
    83	        function push_prop(prop, delay = false) {
    84	                const do_push = () => {
    85	                        const current = props_and_spreads.at(-1);
    86	                        const current_is_props = Array.isArray(current);
    87	                        const props = current_is_props ? current : [];
    88	                        props.push(prop);
    89	                        if (!current_is_props) {
    90	                                props_and_spreads.push(props);
    91	                        }
    92	                };
    93	
    94	                if (delay) {
    95	                        delayed_props.push(do_push);
    96	                } else {
    97	                        do_push();
    98	                }
    99	        }
   100	
   101	        if (slot_scope_applies_to_itself) {
   102	                for (const attribute of node.attributes) {
   103	                        if (attribute.type === 'LetDirective') {
   104	                                lets.push(/** @type {ExpressionStatement} */ (context.visit(attribute)));
   105	                        }
   106	                }
   107	        }
   108	
   109	        for (const attribute of node.attributes) {
   110	                if (attribute.type === 'LetDirective') {
   111	                        if (!slot_scope_applies_to_itself) {
   112	                                lets.push(/** @type {ExpressionStatement} */ (context.visit(attribute, states.default)));
   113	                        }
   114	                } else if (attribute.type === 'OnDirective') {
   115	                        if (!attribute.expression) {
   116	                                context.state.analysis.needs_props = true;
   117	                        }
   118	
   119	                        let handler = build_event_handler(
   120	                                attribute.expression,
   121	                                attribute.metadata.expression,
   122	                                context
   123	                        );
   124	
   125	                        if (attribute.modifiers.includes('once')) {
   126	                                handler = b.call('$.once', handler);
   127	                        }
   128	
   129	                        (events[attribute.name] ||= []).push(handler);
   130	                } else if (attribute.type === 'SpreadAttribute') {
   131	                        const expression = /** @type {Expression} */ (context.visit(attribute));
   132	
   133	                        if (attribute.metadata.expression.has_state) {
   134	                                props_and_spreads.push(
   135	                                        b.thunk(
   136	                                                attribute.metadata.expression.has_await || attribute.metadata.expression.has_call
   137	                                                        ? b.call('$.get', memoizer.add(expression, attribute.metadata.expression.has_await))
   138	                                                        : expression
   139	                                        )
   140	                                );
   141	                        } else {
   142	                                props_and_spreads.push(expression);
   143	                        }
   144	                } else if (attribute.type === 'Attribute') {
   145	                        if (attribute.name.startsWith('--')) {
   146	                                custom_css_props.push(
   147	                                        b.init(
   148	                                                attribute.name,
   149	                                                build_attribute_value(attribute.value, context, (value, metadata) => {
   150	                                                        // TODO put the derived in the local block
   151	                                                        return metadata.has_call || metadata.has_await
   152	                                                                ? b.call('$.get', memoizer.add(value, metadata.has_await))
   153	                                                                : value;
   154	                                                }).value
   155	                                        )
   156	                                );
   157	                                continue;
   158	                        }
   159	
   160	                        if (attribute.name === 'slot') {
   161	                                slot_scope_applies_to_itself = true;
   162	                        }
   163	
   164	                        if (attribute.name === 'children') {
   165	                                has_children_prop = true;
   166	                        }
   167	
   168	                        const { value, has_state } = build_attribute_value(
   169	                                attribute.value,
   170	                                context,
   171	                                (value, metadata) => {
   172	                                        if (!metadata.has_state && !metadata.has_await) return value;
   173	
   174	                                        // When we have a non-simple computation, anything other than an Identifier or Member expression,
   175	                                        // then there's a good chance it needs to be memoized to avoid over-firing when read within the
   176	                                        // child component (e.g. `active={i === index}`)
   177	                                        const should_wrap_in_derived =
   178	                                                metadata.has_await ||
   179	                                                get_attribute_chunks(attribute.value).some((n) => {
   180	                                                        return (
   181	                                                                n.type === 'ExpressionTag' &&
   182	                                                                n.expression.type !== 'Identifier' &&
   183	                                                                n.expression.type !== 'MemberExpression'
   184	                                                        );
   185	                                                });
   186	
   187	                                        return should_wrap_in_derived
   188	                                                ? b.call('$.get', memoizer.add(value, metadata.has_await))
   189	                                                : value;
   190	                                }
   191	                        );
   192	
   193	                        if (has_state) {
   194	                                push_prop(b.get(attribute.name, [b.return(value)]));
   195	                        } else {
   196	                                push_prop(b.init(attribute.name, value));
   197	                        }
   198	                } else if (attribute.type === 'BindDirective') {
   199	                        const expression = /** @type {Expression} */ (context.visit(attribute.expression));
   200	
   201	                        if (
   202	                                dev &&
   203	                                attribute.name !== 'this' &&
   204	                                !is_ignored(node, 'ownership_invalid_binding') &&
   205	                                // bind:x={() => x.y, y => x.y = y} will be handled by the assignment expression binding validation
   206	                                attribute.expression.type !== 'SequenceExpression'
   207	                        ) {
   208	                                const left = object(attribute.expression);
   209	                                const binding = left && context.state.scope.get(left.name);
   210	
   211	                                if (binding?.kind === 'bindable_prop' || binding?.kind === 'prop') {
   212	                                        context.state.analysis.needs_mutation_validation = true;
   213	                                        binding_initializers.push(
   214	                                                b.stmt(
   215	                                                        b.call(
   216	                                                                '$$ownership_validator.binding',
   217	                                                                b.literal(binding.node.name),
   218	                                                                b.id(is_component_dynamic ? intermediate_name : component_name),
   219	                                                                b.thunk(expression)
   220	                                                        )
   221	                                                )
   222	                                        );
   223	                                }
   224	                        }
   225	
   226	                        if (expression.type === 'SequenceExpression') {
   227	                                if (attribute.name === 'this') {
   228	                                        bind_this = attribute.expression;
   229	                                } else {
   230	                                        const [get, set] = expression.expressions;
   231	                                        const get_id = b.id(context.state.scope.generate('bind_get'));
   232	                                        const set_id = b.id(context.state.scope.generate('bind_set'));
   233	
   234	                                        context.state.init.push(b.var(get_id, get));
   235	                                        context.state.init.push(b.var(set_id, set));
   236	
   237	                                        push_prop(b.get(attribute.name, [b.return(b.call(get_id))]));
   238	                                        push_prop(b.set(attribute.name, [b.stmt(b.call(set_id, b.id('$$value')))]));
   239	                                }
   240	                        } else {
   241	                                if (
   242	                                        dev &&
   243	                                        expression.type === 'MemberExpression' &&
   244	                                        context.state.analysis.runes &&
   245	                                        !is_ignored(node, 'binding_property_non_reactive')
   246	                                ) {
   247	                                        validate_binding(context.state, attribute, expression);
   248	                                }
   249	
   250	                                if (attribute.name === 'this') {
   251	                                        bind_this = attribute.expression;
   252	                                } else {
   253	                                        const is_store_sub =
   254	                                                attribute.expression.type === 'Identifier' &&
   255	                                                context.state.scope.get(attribute.expression.name)?.kind === 'store_sub';
   256	
   257	                                        // Delay prop pushes so bindings come at the end, to avoid spreads overwriting them
   258	                                        if (is_store_sub) {
   259	                                                push_prop(
   260	                                                        b.get(attribute.name, [b.stmt(b.call('$.mark_store_binding')), b.return(expression)]),
   261	                                                        true
   262	                                                );
   263	                                        } else {
   264	                                                push_prop(b.get(attribute.name, [b.return(expression)]), true);
   265	                                        }
   266	
   267	                                        const assignment = b.assignment(
   268	                                                '=',
   269	                                                /** @type {Pattern} */ (attribute.expression),
   270	                                                b.id('$$value')
   271	                                        );
   272	
   273	                                        push_prop(
   274	                                                b.set(attribute.name, [b.stmt(/** @type {Expression} */ (context.visit(assignment)))]),
   275	                                                true
   276	                                        );
   277	                                }
   278	                        }
   279	                } else if (attribute.type === 'AttachTag') {
   280	                        const evaluated = context.state.scope.evaluate(attribute.expression);
   281	
   282	                        let expression = /** @type {Expression} */ (context.visit(attribute.expression));
   283	
   284	                        if (attribute.metadata.expression.has_state) {
   285	                                expression = b.arrow(
   286	                                        [b.id('$$node')],
   287	                                        b.call(
   288	                                                evaluated.is_function ? expression : b.logical('||', expression, b.id('$.noop')),
   289	                                                b.id('$$node')
   290	                                        )
   291	                                );
   292	                        }
   293	
   294	                        push_prop(b.prop('init', b.call('$.attachment'), expression, true));
   295	                }
   296	        }
   297	
   298	        delayed_props.forEach((fn) => fn());
   299	
   300	        if (slot_scope_applies_to_itself) {
   301	                context.state.init.push(...lets);
   302	        }
   303	
   304	        if (Object.keys(events).length > 0) {
   305	                const events_expression = b.object(
   306	                        Object.keys(events).map((name) =>
   307	                                b.init(name, events[name].length > 1 ? b.array(events[name]) : events[name][0])
   308	                        )
   309	                );
   310	                push_prop(b.init('$$events', events_expression));
   311	        }
   312	
   313	        /** @type {Statement[]} */
   314	        const snippet_declarations = [];
   315	
   316	        /** @type {import('estree').Property[]} */
   317	        const serialized_slots = [];
   318	
   319	        // Group children by slot
   320	        for (const child of node.fragment.nodes) {
   321	                if (child.type === 'SnippetBlock') {
   322	                        // the SnippetBlock visitor adds a declaration to `init`, but if it's directly
   323	                        // inside a component then we want to hoist them into a block so that they
   324	                        // can be used as props without creating conflicts
   325	                        context.visit(child, {
   326	                                ...context.state,
   327	                                init: snippet_declarations
   328	                        });
   329	
   330	                        push_prop(b.prop('init', child.expression, child.expression));
   331	
   332	                        // Interop: allows people to pass snippets when component still uses slots
   333	                        serialized_slots.push(
   334	                                b.init(child.expression.name === 'children' ? 'default' : child.expression.name, b.true)
   335	                        );
   336	
   337	                        continue;
   338	                }
   339	
   340	                let slot_name = determine_slot(child) ?? 'default';
   341	
   342	                (children[slot_name] ||= []).push(child);
   343	        }
   344	
   345	        // Serialize each slot
   346	        for (const slot_name of Object.keys(children)) {
   347	                const block = /** @type {BlockStatement} */ (
   348	                        context.visit(
   349	                                {
   350	                                        ...node.fragment,
   351	                                        // @ts-expect-error
   352	                                        nodes: children[slot_name]
   353	                                },
   354	                                slot_name === 'default'
   355	                                        ? slot_scope_applies_to_itself
   356	                                                ? context.state
   357	                                                : states.default
   358	                                        : {
   359	                                                        ...context.state,
   360	                                                        scope: node.metadata.scopes[slot_name],
   361	                                                        transform: { ...context.state.transform }
   362	                                                }
   363	                        )
   364	                );
   365	
   366	                if (block.body.length === 0) continue;
   367	
   368	                const slot_fn = b.arrow(
   369	                        [b.id('$$anchor'), b.id('$$slotProps')],
   370	                        b.block([
   371	                                ...(slot_name === 'default' && !slot_scope_applies_to_itself ? lets : []),
   372	                                ...block.body
   373	                        ])
   374	                );
   375	
   376	                if (slot_name === 'default' && !has_children_prop) {
   377	                        if (
   378	                                lets.length === 0 &&
   379	                                children.default.every(
   380	                                        (node) =>
   381	                                                node.type !== 'SvelteFragment' ||
   382	                                                !node.attributes.some((attr) => attr.type === 'LetDirective')
   383	                                )
   384	                        ) {
   385	                                // create `children` prop...
   386	                                push_prop(
   387	                                        b.init(
   388	                                                'children',
   389	                                                dev ? b.call('$.wrap_snippet', b.id(context.state.analysis.name), slot_fn) : slot_fn
   390	                                        )
   391	                                );
   392	
   393	                                // and `$$slots.default: true` so that `<slot>` on the child works
   394	                                serialized_slots.push(b.init(slot_name, b.true));
   395	                        } else {
   396	                                // create `$$slots.default`...
   397	                                serialized_slots.push(b.init(slot_name, slot_fn));
   398	
   399	                                // and a `children` prop that errors
   400	                                push_prop(b.init('children', b.id('$.invalid_default_snippet')));
   401	                        }
   402	                } else {
   403	                        serialized_slots.push(b.init(slot_name, slot_fn));
   404	                }
   405	        }
   406	
   407	        if (serialized_slots.length > 0) {
   408	                push_prop(b.init('$$slots', b.object(serialized_slots)));
   409	        }
   410	
   411	        if (
   412	                !context.state.analysis.runes &&
   413	                node.attributes.some((attribute) => attribute.type === 'BindDirective')
   414	        ) {
   415	                push_prop(b.init('$$legacy', b.true));
   416	        }
   417	
   418	        const props_expression =
   419	                props_and_spreads.length === 0 ||
   420	                (props_and_spreads.length === 1 && Array.isArray(props_and_spreads[0]))
   421	                        ? b.object(/** @type {Property[]} */ (props_and_spreads[0]) || [])
   422	                        : b.call(
   423	                                        '$.spread_props',
   424	                                        ...props_and_spreads.map((p) => (Array.isArray(p) ? b.object(p) : p))
   425	                                );
   426	
   427	        /** @param {Expression} node_id */
   428	        let fn = (node_id) => {
   429	                return b.call(
   430	                        // TODO We can remove this ternary once we remove legacy mode, since in runes mode dynamic components
   431	                        // will be handled separately through the `$.component` function, and then the component name will
   432	                        // always be referenced through just the identifier here.
   433	                        is_component_dynamic
   434	                                ? intermediate_name
   435	                                : /** @type {Expression} */ (context.visit(b.member_id(component_name))),
   436	                        node_id,
   437	                        props_expression
   438	                );
   439	        };
   440	
   441	        if (bind_this !== null) {
   442	                const prev = fn;
   443	
   444	                fn = (node_id) => {
   445	                        return build_bind_this(bind_this, prev(node_id), context);
   446	                };
   447	        }
   448	
   449	        const statements = [...snippet_declarations, ...memoizer.deriveds(context.state.analysis.runes)];
   450	
   451	        if (is_component_dynamic) {
   452	                const prev = fn;
   453	
   454	                fn = (node_id) => {
   455	                        return b.call(
   456	                                '$.component',
   457	                                node_id,
   458	                                b.thunk(
   459	                                        /** @type {Expression} */ (
   460	                                                context.visit(node.type === 'Component' ? b.member_id(component_name) : node.expression)
   461	                                        )
   462	                                ),
   463	                                b.arrow(
   464	                                        [b.id('$$anchor'), b.id(intermediate_name)],
   465	                                        b.block([...binding_initializers, b.stmt(prev(b.id('$$anchor')))])
   466	                                )
   467	                        );
   468	                };
   469	        } else {
   470	                statements.push(...binding_initializers);
   471	        }
   472	
   473	        if (Object.keys(custom_css_props).length > 0) {
   474	                if (context.state.metadata.namespace === 'svg') {
   475	                        // this boils down to <g><!></g>
   476	                        context.state.template.push_element('g', node.start);
   477	                } else {
   478	                        // this boils down to <svelte-css-wrapper style='display: contents'><!></svelte-css-wrapper>
   479	                        context.state.template.push_element('svelte-css-wrapper', node.start);
   480	                        context.state.template.set_prop('style', 'display: contents');
   481	                }
   482	
   483	                context.state.template.push_comment();
   484	                context.state.template.pop_element();
   485	
   486	                statements.push(
   487	                        b.stmt(b.call('$.css_props', anchor, b.thunk(b.object(custom_css_props)))),
   488	                        b.stmt(fn(b.member(anchor, 'lastChild'))),
   489	                        b.stmt(b.call('$.reset', anchor))
   490	                );
   491	        } else {
   492	                context.state.template.push_comment();
   493	
   494	                statements.push(add_svelte_meta(fn(anchor), node, 'component', { componentTag: node.name }));
   495	        }
   496	
   497	        memoizer.apply();
   498	
   499	        const async_values = memoizer.async_values();
   500	
   501	        if (async_values) {
   502	                return b.stmt(
   503	                        b.call(
   504	                                '$.async',
   505	                                anchor,
   506	                                async_values,
   507	                                b.arrow([b.id('$$anchor'), ...memoizer.async_ids()], b.block(statements))
   508	                        )
   509	                );
   510	        }
   511	
   512	        return statements.length > 1 ? b.block(statements) : statements[0];
   513	}
   514

     1	/** @import { Effect, Value } from '#client' */
     2	
     3	import { DESTROYED } from '#client/constants';
     4	import { DEV } from 'esm-env';
     5	import { component_context, is_runes, set_component_context } from '../context.js';
     6	import { get_pending_boundary } from '../dom/blocks/boundary.js';
     7	import { invoke_error_boundary } from '../error-handling.js';
     8	import {
     9	        active_effect,
    10	        active_reaction,
    11	        set_active_effect,
    12	        set_active_reaction
    13	} from '../runtime.js';
    14	import { current_batch, suspend } from './batch.js';
    15	import {
    16	        async_derived,
    17	        current_async_effect,
    18	        derived,
    19	        derived_safe_equal,
    20	        set_from_async_derived
    21	} from './deriveds.js';
    22	import { aborted } from './effects.js';
    23	
    24	/**
    25	 *
    26	 * @param {Array<() => any>} sync
    27	 * @param {Array<() => Promise<any>>} async
    28	 * @param {(values: Value[]) => any} fn
    29	 */
    30	export function flatten(sync, async, fn) {
    31	        const d = is_runes() ? derived : derived_safe_equal;
    32	
    33	        if (async.length === 0) {
    34	                fn(sync.map(d));
    35	                return;
    36	        }
    37	
    38	        var batch = current_batch;
    39	        var parent = /** @type {Effect} */ (active_effect);
    40	
    41	        var restore = capture();
    42	        var boundary = get_pending_boundary();
    43	
    44	        Promise.all(async.map((expression) => async_derived(expression)))
    45	                .then((result) => {
    46	                        batch?.activate();
    47	
    48	                        restore();
    49	
    50	                        try {
    51	                                fn([...sync.map(d), ...result]);
    52	                        } catch (error) {
    53	                                // ignore errors in blocks that have already been destroyed
    54	                                if ((parent.f & DESTROYED) === 0) {
    55	                                        invoke_error_boundary(error, parent);
    56	                                }
    57	                        }
    58	
    59	                        batch?.deactivate();
    60	                        unset_context();
    61	                })
    62	                .catch((error) => {
    63	                        boundary.error(error);
    64	                });
    65	}
    66	
    67	/**
    68	 * Captures the current effect context so that we can restore it after
    69	 * some asynchronous work has happened (so that e.g. `await a + b`
    70	 * causes `b` to be registered as a dependency).
    71	 */
    72	function capture() {
    73	        var previous_effect = active_effect;
    74	        var previous_reaction = active_reaction;
    75	        var previous_component_context = component_context;
    76	
    77	        return function restore() {
    78	                set_active_effect(previous_effect);
    79	                set_active_reaction(previous_reaction);
    80	                set_component_context(previous_component_context);
    81	
    82	                if (DEV) {
    83	                        set_from_async_derived(null);
    84	                }
    85	        };
    86	}
    87	
    88	/**
    89	 * Wraps an `await` expression in such a way that the effect context that was
    90	 * active before the expression evaluated can be reapplied afterwards —
    91	 * `await a + b` becomes `(await $.save(a))() + b`
    92	 * @template T
    93	 * @param {Promise<T>} promise
    94	 * @returns {Promise<() => T>}
    95	 */
    96	export async function save(promise) {
    97	        var restore = capture();
    98	        var value = await promise;
    99	
   100	        return () => {
   101	                restore();
   102	                return value;
   103	        };
   104	}
   105	
   106	/**
   107	 * Reset `current_async_effect` after the `promise` resolves, so
   108	 * that we can emit `await_reactivity_loss` warnings
   109	 * @template T
   110	 * @param {Promise<T>} promise
   111	 * @returns {Promise<() => T>}
   112	 */
   113	export async function track_reactivity_loss(promise) {
   114	        var previous_async_effect = current_async_effect;
   115	        var value = await promise;
   116	
   117	        return () => {
   118	                set_from_async_derived(previous_async_effect);
   119	                return value;
   120	        };
   121	}
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Used in `for await` loops in DEV, so
   125	 * that we can emit `await_reactivity_loss` warnings
   126	 * after each `async_iterator` result resolves and
   127	 * after the `async_iterator` return resolves (if it runs)
   128	 * @template T
   129	 * @template TReturn
   130	 * @param {Iterable<T> | AsyncIterable<T>} iterable
   131	 * @returns {AsyncGenerator<T, TReturn | undefined>}
   132	 */
   133	export async function* for_await_track_reactivity_loss(iterable) {
   134	        // This is based on the algorithms described in ECMA-262:
   135	        // ForIn/OfBodyEvaluation
   136	        // https://tc39.es/ecma262/multipage/ecmascript-language-statements-and-declarations.html#sec-runtime-semantics-forin-div-ofbodyevaluation-lhs-stmt-iterator-lhskind-labelset
   137	        // AsyncIteratorClose
   138	        // https://tc39.es/ecma262/multipage/abstract-operations.html#sec-asynciteratorclose
   139	
   140	        /** @type {AsyncIterator<T, TReturn>} */
   141	        // @ts-ignore
   142	        const iterator = iterable[Symbol.asyncIterator]?.() ?? iterable[Symbol.iterator]?.();
   143	
   144	        if (iterator === undefined) {
   145	                throw new TypeError('value is not async iterable');
   146	        }
   147	
   148	        /** Whether the completion of the iterator was "normal", meaning it wasn't ended via `break` or a similar method */
   149	        let normal_completion = false;
   150	        try {
   151	                while (true) {
   152	                        const { done, value } = (await track_reactivity_loss(iterator.next()))();
   153	                        if (done) {
   154	                                normal_completion = true;
   155	                                break;
   156	                        }
   157	                        yield value;
   158	                }
   159	        } finally {
   160	                // If the iterator had a normal completion and `return` is defined on the iterator, call it and return the value
   161	                if (normal_completion && iterator.return !== undefined) {
   162	                        // eslint-disable-next-line no-unsafe-finally
   163	                        return /** @type {TReturn} */ ((await track_reactivity_loss(iterator.return()))().value);
   164	                }
   165	        }
   166	}
   167	
   168	export function unset_context() {
   169	        set_active_effect(null);
   170	        set_active_reaction(null);
   171	        set_component_context(null);
   172	        if (DEV) set_from_async_derived(null);
   173	}
   174	
   175	/**
   176	 * @param {() => Promise<void>} fn
   177	 */
   178	export async function async_body(fn) {
   179	        const unsuspend = suspend();
   180	        const active = /** @type {Effect} */ (active_effect);
   181	
   182	        try {
   183	                await fn();
   184	        } catch (error) {
   185	                if (!aborted(active)) {
   186	                        invoke_error_boundary(error, active);
   187	                }
   188	        } finally {
   189	                unsuspend();
   190	        }
   191	}

Now I need to understand the issue better. The PR says: "When rendering an awaited variable inside a snippet — without explicitly declaring it in the snippet parameters — the console throws: `Uncaught (in promise) ReferenceError: value is not defined`"

Let me look at how the SnippetBlock handles variables from the parent scope when `has_await` is true. The issue is likely that when a snippet body has `await`, it becomes an async function. But async functions can't access variables from the parent scope in the same way if they're not captured properly.

Let me look at how `#await` expressions are transformed:

     1	/** @import { BlockStatement, Pattern, Statement } from 'estree' */
     2	/** @import { AST } from '#compiler' */
     3	/** @import { ComponentClientTransformState, ComponentContext } from '../types' */
     4	import { extract_identifiers, is_expression_async } from '../../../../utils/ast.js';
     5	import * as b from '#compiler/builders';
     6	import { create_derived } from '../utils.js';
     7	import { get_value } from './shared/declarations.js';
     8	import { build_expression, add_svelte_meta } from './shared/utils.js';
     9	
    10	/**
    11	 * @param {AST.AwaitBlock} node
    12	 * @param {ComponentContext} context
    13	 */
    14	export function AwaitBlock(node, context) {
    15	        context.state.template.push_comment();
    16	
    17	        // Visit {#await <expression>} first to ensure that scopes are in the correct order
    18	        const expression = b.thunk(
    19	                build_expression(context, node.expression, node.metadata.expression),
    20	                node.metadata.expression.has_await
    21	        );
    22	
    23	        let then_block;
    24	        let catch_block;
    25	
    26	        if (node.then) {
    27	                const then_context = {
    28	                        ...context,
    29	                        state: { ...context.state, transform: { ...context.state.transform } }
    30	                };
    31	                const argument = node.value && create_derived_block_argument(node.value, then_context);
    32	
    33	                /** @type {Pattern[]} */
    34	                const args = [b.id('$$anchor')];
    35	                if (argument) args.push(argument.id);
    36	
    37	                const declarations = argument?.declarations ?? [];
    38	                const block = /** @type {BlockStatement} */ (then_context.visit(node.then, then_context.state));
    39	
    40	                then_block = b.arrow(args, b.block([...declarations, ...block.body]));
    41	        }
    42	
    43	        if (node.catch) {
    44	                const catch_context = { ...context, state: { ...context.state } };
    45	                const argument = node.error && create_derived_block_argument(node.error, catch_context);
    46	
    47	                /** @type {Pattern[]} */
    48	                const args = [b.id('$$anchor')];
    49	                if (argument) args.push(argument.id);
    50	
    51	                const declarations = argument?.declarations ?? [];
    52	                const block = /** @type {BlockStatement} */ (
    53	                        catch_context.visit(node.catch, catch_context.state)
    54	                );
    55	
    56	                catch_block = b.arrow(args, b.block([...declarations, ...block.body]));
    57	        }
    58	
    59	        context.state.init.push(
    60	                add_svelte_meta(
    61	                        b.call(
    62	                                '$.await',
    63	                                context.state.node,
    64	                                expression,
    65	                                node.pending
    66	                                        ? b.arrow([b.id('$$anchor')], /** @type {BlockStatement} */ (context.visit(node.pending)))
    67	                                        : b.null,
    68	                                then_block,
    69	                                catch_block
    70	                        ),
    71	                        node,
    72	                        'await'
    73	                )
    74	        );
    75	}
    76	
    77	/**
    78	 * @param {Pattern} node
    79	 * @param {import('zimmerframe').Context<AST.SvelteNode, ComponentClientTransformState>} context
    80	 * @returns {{ id: Pattern, declarations: null | Statement[] }}
    81	 */
    82	function create_derived_block_argument(node, context) {
    83	        if (node.type === 'Identifier') {
    84	                context.state.transform[node.name] = { read: get_value };
    85	                return { id: node, declarations: null };
    86	        }
    87	
    88	        const pattern = /** @type {Pattern} */ (context.visit(node));
    89	        const identifiers = extract_identifiers(node);
    90	
    91	        const id = b.id('$$source');
    92	        const value = b.id('$$value');
    93	
    94	        const block = b.block([
    95	                b.var(pattern, b.call('$.get', id)),
    96	                b.return(b.object(identifiers.map((identifier) => b.prop('init', identifier, identifier))))
    97	        ]);
    98	
    99	        const declarations = [b.var(value, create_derived(context.state, block))];
   100	
   101	        for (const id of identifiers) {
   102	                context.state.transform[id.name] = { read: get_value };
   103	
   104	                declarations.push(
   105	                        b.var(id, create_derived(context.state, b.member(b.call('$.get', value), id)))
   106	                );
   107	        }
   108	
   109	        return { id, declarations };
   110	}
   111
